rationale: In the theory section, Zhao et al. (2007) is cited among key sources establishing the process-vs.-outcome simulation distinction that underpins the citing paper's hypotheses. In the methods, the process simulation measure is explicitly described as adapted in part from Zhao et al. (along with other prior papers), indicating concrete construct operationalization use rather than a passing mention. Although the reference appears in grouped citations, it supports central framing and measurement choices used across the studies, so the citation plays a substantive foundational role.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Zhao et al. (2007) as part of the core conceptual base for process versus outcome mental simulation and uses it when building its process-simulation measurement approach. It then develops new advertising antecedents and boundary conditions on top of that established foundation.
